body.question_1: How would you rate the university's communication with parents regarding your child's academic progress and welfare?
body.answer_1: Communication from the School of Engineering to parents regarding student progress is decent and fulfills the minimum requirements, but falls short of what a genuinely family-supportive institution should provide. The parent portal provides access to basic grade and attendance data, but the interface is somewhat outdated and not particularly intuitive for parents who are not technology-confident. Proactive outreach — for example, early alerts when a student's performance or attendance begins to decline — is currently limited and reactive. More frequent, personalized, and timely communication from the Academic Affairs Office would significantly strengthen the partnership between parents and the institution in supporting student success.

body.question_2: How satisfied are you with the quality of education your child is receiving at this university?
body.answer_2: The teaching quality in my Chemical Engineering courses at the School of Engineering has been remarkable this semester. My lecturers consistently demonstrate deep subject matter expertise and present complex material in a clear, well-structured, and engaging manner that truly enhances comprehension. I particularly commend the integration of real-world industry examples and hands-on exercises into every session, which makes abstract theoretical concepts genuinely applicable. The interactive teaching style — characterized by open discussions, collaborative problem-solving, and prompt feedback on assignments — has significantly accelerated my academic growth. The faculty's visible dedication and responsiveness to students makes me confident I am receiving a high-calibre education that will serve me well in my future career.

body.question_3: How confident are you in the university's ability to prepare your child for future career opportunities?
body.answer_3: Career preparation support from the Career Center at the School of Engineering has been inconsistent and has left many students — including myself — feeling ill-equipped for the competitive graduate employment market. Career workshops are infrequent, poorly promoted, and often scheduled at times that conflict with lecture commitments, reducing student participation. The internship database is outdated and contains many positions that are no longer active, and support for students during internship placements is minimal to nonexistent. I urge the university to substantially reinvest in the Career Center — expanding its employer network, modernizing its tools, and increasing the frequency and quality of career development programming across all academic programs and year levels.

body.question_4: How would you assess the campus safety measures and student welfare programs in place at this institution?
body.answer_4: My confidence in the School of Engineering's ability to ensure insufficient levels of campus safety and student welfare has been significantly diminished by several incidents and structural shortcomings reported to me by my child. Security coverage appears insufficient in certain areas of campus, particularly during late evening hours, and lighting in several pathways is inadequate and has been reported without timely resolution. Mental health support services are critically under-resourced — my child was advised to wait over three weeks for a counseling appointment during a period of acute personal difficulty, which is simply unacceptable. I urge the Student Affairs Office to treat student welfare as a non-negotiable priority and invest accordingly, without further delay.

body.question_5: How transparent and straightforward have you found the university's financial policies, fee management, and scholarship communication?
body.answer_5: Financial management by the Finance Department at the School of Engineering has been adequate from my perspective as a parent, meeting the fundamental requirements of tuition billing and payment processing without notable excellence or failure. Fee statements are generally accurate, though occasional discrepancies have required follow-up that is more time-consuming than it should be. Communication about scholarship timelines could be significantly improved — families benefit greatly from advance notice when disbursement dates shift or eligibility criteria change, and this is currently lacking. I would welcome a dedicated parent-facing financial FAQ and communication channel to reduce the need for direct contact for routine financial queries.

body.question_6: How effective do you find the parent portal and digital communication systems provided by the university?
body.answer_6: The parent portal at the School of Engineering, managed by the IT Support Department, is disappointing in its functionality, reliability, and user experience, and has been more of a source of frustration than a useful communication tool. The interface is dated and difficult to navigate, with key information such as grade details and attendance records buried under multiple menu layers. The system experiences frequent downtime — often during critical periods such as grade release and enrollment — and error messages provide no useful guidance for resolving access issues independently. I strongly point out a complete redesign of the parent portal with a focus on intuitive UX, mobile compatibility, and real-time data synchronization to bring it in line with modern digital standards.

body.question_7: How satisfied are you with the academic counseling and advising support your child receives?
body.answer_7: Academic counseling services at the School of Engineering have been concerning in their availability, quality, and proactivity from my perspective as a parent observing my child's experience this academic year. Appointments with academic advisors are difficult to secure, often requiring weeks of waiting for a short session that does not always result in actionable or personalized guidance. The advice provided is sometimes generic and does not demonstrate familiarity with the student's individual history, goals, or challenges. I strongly point out the Academic Affairs Office to increase its counseling staff capacity, invest in advisor training, and implement proactive outreach to students who may be struggling academically before their situations deteriorate into formal academic difficulties.

body.question_8: How well does the university address students' mental health and personal well-being needs?
body.answer_8: The student support services offered by the Student Affairs Office at the School of Engineering have been outstanding and have made a genuine difference to my overall well-being and university experience throughout the semester. Counseling services are readily accessible, professionally delivered, and clearly communicated to students through multiple channels including the student portal, orientation sessions, and notice boards. The range of support available — from academic counseling to mental health resources and financial hardship assistance — reflects a comprehensive understanding of the diverse challenges students face during their academic journey. I also praise the regular student welfare check-ins organized by the office, which foster a sense of community and belonging on campus.

body.question_9: How would you evaluate the quality and suitability of the university's physical facilities and campus environment?
body.answer_9: Campus facilities at the School of Engineering, maintained by the Facilities Management, are generally acceptable and adequate for day-to-day academic activities, though several areas require attention and investment to meet growing needs. Core academic spaces — including lecture halls and laboratories — are functional and reasonably maintained, though some equipment is beginning to show age and inconsistency in performance. Student study and relaxation spaces are insufficient during peak periods, and the condition of older campus buildings varies considerably. A systematic facilities audit followed by a prioritized improvement plan would help address longstanding issues and ensure the campus environment supports the academic ambitions of all students more effectively.

body.question_10: What improvements would you suggest to enhance the university's support for both students and their families?
body.answer_10: As a parent who has closely observed my child's experience at the School of Engineering over the past year, I can say with genuine satisfaction that the institution has provided an remarkable educational and personal development environment that has exceeded our family's expectations in most meaningful respects. The combination of academic rigor, caring faculty, comprehensive student support, and strong career preparation has resulted in visible and meaningful growth in my child's competence, confidence, and professional readiness. I would enthusiastically recommend this university to other families seeking a high-quality, student-centered tertiary education for their children, and I look forward to continuing to partner with the institution as my child completes the remainder of their degree.
